rationale: The initial alert (A0) shows `cmd.exe` being spawned by `java.exe` to download and execute `qSwUwejx.exe` from a suspicious IP (10.0.2.13) using `certutil.exe` (EV-003, EV-004). This downloaded executable then initiates a network connection to the same suspicious IP on port 4444 (EV-007), indicating potential command and control. Subsequently, `qSwUwejx.exe` spawns `cmd.exe` (EV-008, EV-009), which then uses `certutil.exe` to download `AnyDesk.exe` from a legitimate domain to a temporary directory (EV-011, EV-012), suggesting an attempt to establish remote access. The combination of suspicious download, C2 communication, and subsequent download of a remote access tool strongly indicates malicious activity.
